Base Maps

Base maps are the foundational geographic layers used to provide spatial context in miraX. They do not contain operational data but serve as a visual reference for overlaying analytical layers such as sensors, models, and GeoJSON data.

Different base maps are optimized for specific tasks, such as navigation, terrain analysis, or high-density data visualisation.

Available Base Maps

Basemap Description Best Used For
OpenStreetMap Roads and reference mapping Navigation
Satellite Imagery High resolution imagery Visual inspection
Terrain Elevation-focused basemap Topography
Light Map Minimal cartographic background Data overlays
Dark Map Dark themed basemap Dense thematic layers
Topographic Map Detailed terrain/cartography Terrain analysis
Hydrological Map Rivers and water features Water systems

Basemap Comparison

OpenStreetMap

ESRI Satellite

Google Map

Basemap Labels 3D Support Best for Data Overlays
OpenStreetMap Yes No Medium
Satellite No Yes High
Terrain Yes Yes High
Light Yes No Very High
Dark Yes No Very High

Features

  • Basemap switching
  • 2D and 3D support
  • Terrain visualization
  • Background context for layers

How to Choose a Basemap

Selecting the appropriate basemap depends on your use case:

  • Use Light or Dark maps when working with many data layers
  • Use Satellite imagery for real-world visual validation
  • Use Terrain or Topographic maps for elevation and landscape analysis
  • Use Hydrological maps for water-related studies
  • Use OpenStreetMap for navigation and general reference
